concept:
Tested 5 2006/03/09 19:00
Patient with headache:
Unilateral
Severe
Periorbital
with tearing
with conjunctival erythema is referred to as: A cluster heachache
Prophylactic treatment for migrain:
Beta blockers
Calcium channel blockers
TCAs
What is the most common pituitary tumor? What is its treatment?
Most common pituitary tumor: Prolactinoma
Treatment: Dopamine agents (DA) (e.g. bromocriptine)
A 55 year-old patient presents with acute "broken speech." What type of aphasia? What lobe and vascular distribution? First step in workup?
Aphasia type:Broca's aphasia
Lobe:Frontal
Vascular distribution: Left middle cerebral artery (MCA)
Workup: Head CT to rule out a hemmorhage
Most common two causes of SAH are:
Trauma
Berry aneurysm
Crecent-shaped hyperdensity on CT that does not cross the midline is: Subdural hematoma
Patient presents with a history significant for initial altered mental status with an intervening lucid interval. Diagnosis? Most likely etiology? Treatment?
Diagnosis:Epidural hematoma
Etiology:Middle meningeal artery
Treatment:Neurosurgical evacuation
CSF findings with SAH are:
Elevated ICP
RBCs
Xanthochromia
Albuminocytologic dissociation is: Guillain-Barre (increased protein in CSF with only modest increase in cell count)
A 61 year-old patient in the ICU is awake and alert but cannot move anything but the eyes and eyelids. This state is called: "Locked-in Syndrome"
Cold water is flushed into a patient's ear, and the fast phase of the nystagmus is toward the opposite side. Is this normal or pathological? Normal Abnormal
Most common primary sources of metastases to the brain are:
Lung
Breast
Skin (melanoma)
Kidney
GI tract
Dysequilibrium associated with tinnitus and hearing loss and often treated with acetazolamide is: Meniere's disease
Most frequent presentation of intracranial neoplasm is: Headache
Most common causes of seizures in children (age 2-10 years) are:
Infection
Febrile seizures
Trauma
Idiopathic
Most common causes of seizures in young adults (age 18-35 years) are:
Trauma
Alchohol withdrawal
Brain tumor
Mainstay of treatment for myasthenia gravis is: Immunosuppressants (e.g. prednisone)
First line medication for status epilepticus is: IV benzodiazepine
Most common intracranial malignancy is: Metastatic
What percentage lesion is an indication for carotid endarterectomy? 70% if stenosis is symptomatic
Most common causes of dementia are:
Alzheimer's
Multi-infarct
Atrophy of the mammillary bodies is: Wernicke's encephalopathy
Combined upper and lower motor neuron disorder is: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)
Rigidity and stiffness with resting tremor and masked facies is: Parkinson's disease
Mainstay of Parkinson's therapy is: Levodopa/carbidopa
Treatment for Guillain-Barre syndrome is: IVIG or plasmapheresis
Rigidity
Stiffness that progresses to choreiform movements accompanied by:
moodiness
altered behavior
indicates: Huntington's disease
Patient:
is 6 year-old
is female
with a port wine stain in the V2 distribution
has mental retardation
has seizures
has leptomeningeal angioma indicating:
Diagnosis:Sturge-Weber syndrome
Treatment:Treat symptomatically. Possible focal cerebral resection of affected lobe
Cafe-au-lait spots on the skin indicate: Neurofibromatosis 1
Hyperphagia
Hypersexuality
Hyperorality
Hyperdocility indicate: Kluver-Bucy syndrome (amygdala)
What do you administer to a symptomatic patient to diagnose myasthenia gravis? Edrophonium
